text: By 2035, we may see the first true Artificial General Intelligence, or AGI. Unlike today's narrow AI, which can only perform specific tasks, AGI would possess human-like general intelligence, able to learn and reason across any domain. Proponents argue that AGI could solve complex global problems, from curing diseases to managing climate change. However, risks remain significant: job displacement, ethical misuse, or even loss of control. Experts disagree on timelines, but many predict AGI could emerge between 2040 and 2050. Ultimately, how we develop and regulate this technology will determine whether it becomes our greatest tool or our biggest challenge.
